detaileddescription.para:
slice information
SliceInfo Generated from class MR::SliceInfoBase. Derived classes: Direct: (non-virtual) MR::SliceInfo Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

slice information these fields will be ignored in sorting
SliceInfoBase Generated from class MR::SliceInfo. Base classes: Direct: (non-virtual) MR::SliceInfoBase Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

instance number Returns a pointer to a member variable of class MR::SliceInfoBase named instanceNum. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. The reference to this object might be preserved as the return value.

instance number Modifies a member variable of class MR::SliceInfoBase named instanceNum. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. When this function is called, this object will drop object references it held previously in instanceNum.

instance number Returns a mutable pointer to a member variable of class MR::SliceInfoBase named instanceNum. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. The reference to this object might be preserved as the return value.

layer height Returns a pointer to a member variable of class MR::SliceInfoBase named z. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. The reference to this object might be preserved as the return value.

layer height Modifies a member variable of class MR::SliceInfoBase named z. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. When this function is called, this object will drop object references it held previously in z.

layer height Returns a mutable pointer to a member variable of class MR::SliceInfoBase named z. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. The reference to this object might be preserved as the return value.

file index Returns a pointer to a member variable of class MR::SliceInfoBase named fileNum. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. The reference to this object might be preserved as the return value.

file index Modifies a member variable of class MR::SliceInfoBase named fileNum. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. When this function is called, this object will drop object references it held previously in fileNum.

file index Returns a mutable pointer to a member variable of class MR::SliceInfoBase named fileNum. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. The reference to this object might be preserved as the return value.
Constructs an empty (default-constructed) instance. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_SliceInfoBase_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Constructs an array of empty (default-constructed) instances, of the specified size. Will never return null. The array must be destroyed using MR_SliceInfoBase_DestroyArray(). Use MR_SliceInfoBase_OffsetMutablePtr() and MR_SliceInfoBase_OffsetPtr() to access the array elements.

Constructs MR::SliceInfoBase elementwise. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_SliceInfoBase_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it. When this function is called, this object will drop any object references it held previously.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Downcasts an instance of MR::SliceInfoBase to a derived class MR::SliceInfo. This is a static downcast, it trusts the programmer that the target type is correct. Results in UB and returns an invalid pointer otherwise. This version is acting on mutable pointers. The reference to the parameter object might be preserved in the return value.

Downcasts an instance of MR::SliceInfoBase to a derived class MR::SliceInfo. This is a static downcast, it trusts the programmer that the target type is correct. Results in UB and returns an invalid pointer otherwise. The reference to the parameter object might be preserved in the return value.

Generated from constructor MR::SliceInfoBase::SliceInfoBase. Parameter _other can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to things referred to by the parameter _other (if any) might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_SliceInfoBase_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

briefdescription.para: Destroys a heap-allocated instance of MR_SliceInfoBase. Does nothing if the pointer is null.
Destroys a heap-allocated array of MR_SliceInfoBase. Does nothing if the pointer is null.

detaileddescription.para: Generated from method MR::SliceInfoBase::operator=. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter _other can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to things referred to by the parameter _other (if any) might be preserved in this object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. When this function is called, this object will drop any object references it held previously.

image position Returns a pointer to a member variable of class MR::SliceInfo named imagePos. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. The reference to this object might be preserved as the return value.

image position Modifies a member variable of class MR::SliceInfo named imagePos. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to the parameter value might be preserved in this object in element imagePos. When this function is called, this object will drop object references it held previously in imagePos.

image position Returns a mutable pointer to a member variable of class MR::SliceInfo named imagePos. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. The reference to this object might be preserved as the return value.

instance number Returns a pointer to a member variable of class MR::SliceInfo named instanceNum. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. The reference to this object might be preserved as the return value.

instance number Modifies a member variable of class MR::SliceInfo named instanceNum. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. When this function is called, this object will drop object references it held previously in instanceNum.

instance number Returns a mutable pointer to a member variable of class MR::SliceInfo named instanceNum. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. The reference to this object might be preserved as the return value.

layer height Returns a pointer to a member variable of class MR::SliceInfo named z. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. The reference to this object might be preserved as the return value.

layer height Modifies a member variable of class MR::SliceInfo named z. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. When this function is called, this object will drop object references it held previously in z.

layer height Returns a mutable pointer to a member variable of class MR::SliceInfo named z. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. The reference to this object might be preserved as the return value.

file index Returns a pointer to a member variable of class MR::SliceInfo named fileNum. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. The reference to this object might be preserved as the return value.

file index Modifies a member variable of class MR::SliceInfo named fileNum. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. When this function is called, this object will drop object references it held previously in fileNum.

file index Returns a mutable pointer to a member variable of class MR::SliceInfo named fileNum. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. The reference to this object might be preserved as the return value.
Constructs an empty (default-constructed) instance. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_SliceInfo_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Constructs an array of empty (default-constructed) instances, of the specified size. Will never return null. The array must be destroyed using MR_SliceInfo_DestroyArray(). Use MR_SliceInfo_OffsetMutablePtr() and MR_SliceInfo_OffsetPtr() to access the array elements.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.

Offsets a pointer to an array element by i positions (not bytes). Use only if you're certain that the pointer points to an array element. The reference to the parameter ptr might be preserved in the return value.
Upcasts an instance of MR::SliceInfo to its base class MR::SliceInfoBase. This version is acting on mutable pointers. The reference to the parameter object might be preserved in the return value.
Upcasts an instance of MR::SliceInfo to its base class MR::SliceInfoBase. The reference to the parameter object might be preserved in the return value.

Generated from constructor MR::SliceInfo::SliceInfo. Parameter _other can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to things referred to by the parameter _other (if any) might be preserved in the constructed object. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_SliceInfo_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

briefdescription.para: Destroys a heap-allocated instance of MR_SliceInfo. Does nothing if the pointer is null.
Destroys a heap-allocated array of MR_SliceInfo. Does nothing if the pointer is null.

detaileddescription.para: Generated from method MR::SliceInfo::operator=. Parameter _this can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter _other can not be null. It is a single object. The reference to things referred to by the parameter _other (if any) might be preserved in this object. The returned pointer will never be null. It is non-owning, do NOT destroy it. When this function is called, this object will drop any object references it held previously.

Sort scan files in given vector by given slice information Generated from function MR::sortScansByOrder. Parameter scans can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter zOrder can not be null. It is a single object.

Read layer heights from given scan file names Generated from function MR::putScanFileNameInZ. Parameter scans can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter zOrder can not be null. It is a single object.
Sort scan files in given vector by names (respect numbers in it) Generated from function MR::sortScanFilesByName. Parameter scans can not be null. It is a single object.
